title: Corone agrestina
taxonomy: Corone agrestina, also known as the green bee-eater or little green bee-eater, is a species of near passerine bird in the family Meropidae. It is a member of the genus Corone, which is a group of birds known for their bright plumage and distinctive calls. The Meropidae family is part of the larger order Coraciiformes, which includes kingfishers, bee-eaters, and rollers. These birds are characterized by their long, curved beaks and bright, often iridescent feathers. The Corone agrestina is native to sub-Saharan Africa, where it can be found in a variety of habitats, including savannas and grasslands.

side_effects: Corone agrestina, also known as Corn Crake, is a plant that has potential side effects due to its toxicity.
